strands.title: Working Directory State
strands.content: User has established /home/tony/projects as a working directory in cycle 1. This binding remains active unless explicitly modified.
key_claims: This directory persists as a context root for file operations

strands.title: Model Configuration
strands.content: User issued /model command in cycle 2 (turn 5), resulting in model selection change to claude-opus-4-6 (Opus). This is the active model for subsequent processing.
key_claims: Active model is now Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6)
Model was explicitly selected via /model command

declared_losses.why: Original context from cycle 1 did not contain explicit intent articulation; this remains unresolved from prior cycle

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specifics of prior model configuration (if any existed before /model command)
declared_losses.why: No prior model state was recorded in cycle 1 tensor; /model command changed state but baseline is unknown

open_questions: What is the user's intent for adding ~/projects as a working directory? (carries forward from cycle 1)
Does the user expect this directory to persist across sessions or is it temporary? (carries forward from cycle 1)
What role should permissions management play in subsequent operations? (carries forward from cycle 1)
Why did the user switch to Opus model — is this for a specific task requirement or exploration?
Will subsequent operations rely on the Opus model capabilities for particular reasoning patterns?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "Maintain binding of ~/projects to /home/tony/projects — this is the active working directory context",
  "Assume Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6) is the active model for this and subsequent reasoning cycles",
  "If user references file operations, use /home/tony/projects as the context root unless explicitly overridden",
  "If user issues additional /model commands, update the active model state accordingly",
  "If user asks about model choice or capabilities, Opus is optimized for complex reasoning — note this in response if relevant",
